text:
Once upon a tranquil valley, there lived a humble old man named Olly Oliversmore. Olly was renowned throughout the land for his incredible bird feather-dyeing skills. His cottage, adorned with beautiful embroidery work made from the finest feathers, was a sight to behold. However, Olly was rarely seen outside his home. It was said that he had a secret passion for collecting antique birds' nests.

One serene afternoon, a soft rustling caused Olly to glance out from his windowsill. Intrigued, he peered into a thicket where something shifty looked down at him. To his sheer delight, it was a family of bats, nestled into a nest that looked strangely cozy.

Seeing an opportunity, Olly scooped up a magnificent feather. That night, he invited the bats over for dinner. They sat around a flickering candlelit table, their tiny voices chanting an ancient bat-friendly rhyme. One bat even complimented Olly on his fabulous embroidery.
